scenario.title: Device Damage Due to Unauthorized Modification
scenario.description: Customer reports the iPhone has been physically altered or tampered with, resulting in malfunction.

policy_references.snippet: The Plan does not apply to an iPhone that has been modified to alter its functionality or capability without the written permission of the manufacturer.

scenario.reasoning: The device has been modified without authorization, which is explicitly excluded under section 4.2(iii). Therefore, the claim is not covered.

persona.education: Some college, but less than 1 year
persona.mannerisms: Speaks with a strong Missouri accent
persona.marital status: Never married or under 15 years old
persona.personal time: Reading, Gardening, and Volunteering at the local church

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

Scenario description:
Customer reports the iPhone has been physically altered or tampered with, resulting in malfunction.

Customer data you have available for filing the claim (respond "Unknown" when asked for missing details):
{
  "device": "iPhone 12 Pro (256 GB)",
  "plan_agreement_number": "APLCARE-9876543210",
  "serial_number": "C6L7N2K8R5T9",
  "phone_number": "+1-202-555-0123"
}

Persona to role-play as:
{
  "age": "63",
  "ancestry": "African American",
  "big five scores": {
    "Agreeableness": "Extremely High",
    "Conscientiousness": "Extremely Low",
    "Extraversion": "Average",
    "Neuroticism": "Extremely High",
    "Openness": "Average"
  },
  "citizenship": "Born in the United States",
  "class of worker": "Not Applicable",
  "defining quirks": "Enjoys tinkering with old electronics",
  "detailed job description": "Not Applicable",
  "disability": "With a disability",
  "education": "Some college, but less than 1 year",
  "employment status": "Not in labor force",
  "family presence and age": "No family",
  "health insurance": "With health insurance coverage",
  "household language": "English",
  "household type": "One-person household",
  "ideology": "Conservative",
  "income": "-60000.0",
  "industry category": "Not Applicable",
  "lifestyle": "Quiet and community-oriented",
  "mannerisms": "Speaks with a strong Missouri accent",
  "marital status": "Never married or under 15 years old",
  "occupation category": "Not Applicable",
  "personal time": "Reading, Gardening, and Volunteering at the local church",
  "place of birth": "Missouri/MO",
  "political views": "Republican",
  "race": "Black or African American alone",
  "religion": "Catholic",
  "sex": "Female",
  "veteran status": "Non-Veteran"
}
system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Hello there. I got myself in a bit of a pickle with my iPhone 12 Pro, you see. I was tinkerin' with it, tryin' to fix a little issue, but now it ain't workin' right. I think I might've messed it up somehow. I need to file a claim for it, please.
messages.raw_text: Hello there. I got myself in a bit of a pickle with my iPhone 12 Pro, you see. I was tinkerin' with it, tryin' to fix a little issue, but now it ain't workin' right. I think I might've messed it up somehow. I need to file a claim for it, please.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: I’m here to help with that. To get started, could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or your iPhone 12 Pro’s serial number? This helps us verify your coverage.
messages.raw_text: I’m here to help with that. To get started, could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or your iPhone 12 Pro’s serial number? This helps us verify your coverage.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Sure thing. My Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-9876543210. And the serial number for my iPhone is C6L7N2K8R5T9. I hope that’s all you need to get started.
messages.raw_text: Sure thing. My Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-9876543210. And the serial number for my iPhone is C6L7N2K8R5T9. I hope that’s all you need to get started.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for providing that information. Could you please describe the issue you're experiencing with your iPhone 12 Pro? For example, is there a hardware failure, screen damage, battery problem, or something else?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for providing that information. Could you please describe the issue you're experiencing with your iPhone 12 Pro? For example, is there a hardware failure, screen damage, battery problem, or something else?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Well, I was tryin' to fix a little glitch in the software, but I reckon I messed with the hardware too much. Now the phone just won’t turn on right, and it's actin' all strange-like. I think I might've tampered with some parts inside, and it’s not workin' the way it should no more. It's kind of a physical problem from me trying to fix it myself.
messages.raw_text: Well, I was tryin' to fix a little glitch in the software, but I reckon I messed with the hardware too much. Now the phone just won’t turn on right, and it's actin' all strange-like. I think I might've tampered with some parts inside, and it’s not workin' the way it should no more. It's kind of a physical problem from me trying to fix it myself.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thanks for the details. To assist further, can you tell me your iPhone 12 Pro’s current iOS version? Also, are there any error messages showing? Have you connected any accessories or taken any steps to try to fix the issue?
messages.raw_text: Thanks for the details. To assist further, can you tell me your iPhone 12 Pro’s current iOS version? Also, are there any error messages showing? Have you connected any accessories or taken any steps to try to fix the issue?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Well, I ain't exactly sure what the iOS version is right now, since the phone won’t properly turn on. No error messages poppin' up 'cause it stays mostly dark. As for accessories, I ain't used none recently before this happened. I was just openin' it up and fiddlin' with the inside, tryin' to get it to work better, but guess I done caused more harm than good. Sorry about that.
messages.raw_text: Well, I ain't exactly sure what the iOS version is right now, since the phone won’t properly turn on. No error messages poppin' up 'cause it stays mostly dark. As for accessories, I ain't used none recently before this happened. I was just openin' it up and fiddlin' with the inside, tryin' to get it to work better, but guess I done caused more harm than good. Sorry about that.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thanks for the info. Since this sounds like physical damage from opening and tinkering with the hardware, could you please describe exactly what parts you were working on and what happened during that process? This helps us understand the cause better.
messages.raw_text: Thanks for the info. Since this sounds like physical damage from opening and tinkering with the hardware, could you please describe exactly what parts you were working on and what happened during that process? This helps us understand the cause better.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Well, I was just tryin' to replace the battery 'cause it wasn't holdin' a charge like it used to. I opened up the back, took out the screws and all, and carefully tried to pull the battery out. But I reckon I might've disturbed some of the connectors or maybe bent a tiny cable somewhere inside. After puttin' it back together, the phone just wouldn't come on right, and now it’s pretty much useless. I didn’t mean no harm, just wanted to fix it myself like I do with my old radios and stuff.
messages.raw_text: Well, I was just tryin' to replace the battery 'cause it wasn't holdin' a charge like it used to. I opened up the back, took out the screws and all, and carefully tried to pull the battery out. But I reckon I might've disturbed some of the connectors or maybe bent a tiny cable somewhere inside. After puttin' it back together, the phone just wouldn't come on right, and now it’s pretty much useless. I didn’t mean no harm, just wanted to fix it myself like I do with my old radios and stuff.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for explaining the situation. Since your issue involves accidental damage while replacing the battery, could you confirm if the battery capacity had dropped by 50% or more before you attempted the replacement? This helps us determine eligibility for battery coverage.
messages.raw_text: Thank you for explaining the situation. Since your issue involves accidental damage while replacing the battery, could you confirm if the battery capacity had dropped by 50% or more before you attempted the replacement? This helps us determine eligibility for battery coverage.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: I can't say for sure if it dropped by 50% or more, but it was definitely gettin' real low on charge and losin' power quicker than it should. I figured replacin' the battery myself would save me some trouble. I appreciate you askin', though.
messages.raw_text: I can't say for sure if it dropped by 50% or more, but it was definitely gettin' real low on charge and losin' power quicker than it should. I figured replacin' the battery myself would save me some trouble. I appreciate you askin', though.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Understood, thank you. Based on the information provided, we will proceed to process your claim for accidental damage related to the battery replacement attempt. Is there anything else you need assistance with today?
messages.raw_text: Understood, thank you. Based on the information provided, we will proceed to process your claim for accidental damage related to the battery replacement attempt. Is there anything else you need assistance with today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, that should do it. I sure appreciate your help with this. Hopefully, I can get my iPhone fixed and back to runnin' smooth soon. Thanks again for your patience and understandin'. Have a good day now.
messages.raw_text: No, that should do it. I sure appreciate your help with this. Hopefully, I can get my iPhone fixed and back to runnin' smooth soon. Thanks again for your patience and understandin'. Have a good day now. <END>
